title: Document From<ParseError> line>0 sentinel in impl
description: From<ParseError> for SieveError (parse_error.rs:65-77) branches on e.line > 0 to choose between SieveErrorKind::Lex and SieveErrorKind::Parse. This logic is only comprehensible if the reader already knows that the form parser always emits line:0 as a sentinel for 'no position'. That invariant is documented on ParseError's struct doc comment, not on the From impl. A reader modifying the From impl without reading the struct docs will not understand why 0 is the branch condition. The impl should carry a brief inline comment explaining the sentinel, or — better — the ParseError struct doc should be referenced explicitly.
